title: bromus erectus subsp. fibrosus
intro: Bromus erectus subsp. fibrosus, also known as fibrous brome, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of fibrous brome include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Fibrous brome can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
